content: Just as human language learners use explicit reflexion (the vocabulary-learned metacognitive skill of reflecting on mistakes and misunderstandings) to reinforce long-term learning, large language models can benefit from grounding their self-reflection mechanisms in the structured, graded reflexion frameworks used in K-12 and higher ed language learning curricula. This turns LLM self-reflection from an ad-hoc technical hack into a systematically designed sequential skill building process that mirrors how humans learn to improve their own output.
